summary: Clean, balanced soundboard capture of a solid ’85 set featuring vintage Dark Star jams and a standout Terrapin Station jam.
review: The second‑set recording is praised as the cleanest and most balanced version available, suggesting a high‑quality soundboard source. Musically the show is regarded as strong – the post‑Terrapin jam (dubbed a “Mind Left Body Jam”), a full‑on Spanish Jam, and vintage jams into and out of Dark Star give this 1985 performance an interesting flavor despite not being an epic summer show.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviews consistently note good to great musical moments and a clean, balanced recording; average user stars are 3½, leading to a solid above‑average rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Described as the cleanest and most balanced copy; no major flaws noted, though one reviewer mentions it was chosen for better tracking without direct comparison.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Set praised for strong jams—especially the post‑Terrapin “Mind Left Body” jam and vintage Dark Star transitions—giving the show an engaging, flowing second set.
